text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its external behavior. Instead of adding new features, you focus on making the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. For example, you might rename confusing variables, break long functions into smaller ones, or remove duplicate sections. The goal is to reduce technical debt—the hidden cost of messy code that slows down future development. By refactoring regularly, you keep the codebase simple and manageable, which helps teams work faster and with fewer bugs. Remember, if it works but is hard to understand, it’s probably time to refactor.
